Denise Lyons, 70, of Michigan, spoke at our [ ]Town Hall of her inability to retire from nursing or get the care she needs for multiple serious health issues. “I was diagnosed with diabetes and the second stage of kidney failure,” she said. “Now, she’s putting off biopsies and canceled her appointments with the cardiovascular specialist and the dentist because of high copays.” “I know putting off care is dangerous, but I can’t afford treatment or to go into more debt,” Denise told us. Kristin Noreen, 59, of Washington, [ ]told us of her harrowing experience of being hit by a car while riding her bike in 2010, which led to traumatic brain injury, getting her hand reattached, and barely survived. “Like anyone, I thought I would be set for life,” she said. “If I’d have known how it was going to be, I might have given up. I’m glad I didn’t, but there’s no end in sight for this debt cycle.” Insurance failed to cover 100 percent of the $1 million total and subsequent care, so Kristin racked up $60k on credit cards. “I sold my home, my retirement account is gone, and I declared bankruptcy,” she told us. “I’m back up to $10k in debt, and I can’t work as much as I need,” Kristin said. “My parents took out a home equity loan, my friend paid for some dental care I needed. it’s a vicious cycle.” ★ ★ ★ [ ][IMG] “Sharing your stories is so important,” Ro Khanna told our members. “Congress members across the country need to realize that medical debt affects real people — it’s affecting their constituents and they should sign on.” Do you owe money for medical care that you couldn’t afford? Have you put life expenses on credit cards to pay medical bills? Share your story! Our goal is to feature voices from every congressional district in America. Join the hundreds of people already speaking out through our survey. [ ]TELL YOUR STORY ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ Bernie, AOC, and Our Revolution are all-in for Helen Gym for Philly Mayor! Our Philly members will join them for a rally for Helen in Philadelphia on Sunday, hoping to repeat the magic progressives pulled off to elect new Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson last month. “Just like we saw in Chicago, this contest in Philadelphia will come down to big money versus grassroots organizing,” Joseph Geevarghese, executive director of Our Revolution, told [ ]The Hill this week. The Hill noted that Our Revolution is getting to work, putting our “mobilization muscle of 40,000 members in the city behind Gym.” And now, Helen is polling in the lead of this crowded race! “What we know is that progressives’ messaging on issues from public safety to education to good jobs and housing has resonated with the Democratic base in key cities, from Chicago to St. Louis,” Joseph said. “We are confident that this message, championed by Helen Gym, will resonate with Philadelphians on election day.” A former public school teacher and community activist, Helen said on our recent [ ]Live Call: “When we win on the ground in our cities, that’s the blueprint, because we cannot wait for Congress.” It’s time to turn out progressive super voters in Philadelphia, where we’ll be mobilizing our 42k members and making 300k voter contacts in the city. “Our theory of change,” Joseph said, “is if we can get that slice of the electorate out, through multiple contacts, that can be a game changer.” [ ]JOIN OUR ELECT PROGRESSIVES TEAM ★ ★ ★ Also in Philly, Our Revolution is proudly supporting Amanda McIllmurray, a working-class organizer who would be the first openly queer Council Member ever in the city! In 2016, Amanda was working as a nanny, a law firm clerk, and a softball umpire when she began organizing for Bernie Sander's presidential campaign and building local progressive power. “I’m running to win good jobs, affordable housing, worker’s rights, and universal family care,” Amanda said. As the daughter of union members, she knows “everyone deserves the chance to have just ONE job, a family-sustaining job.” ★ ★ ★ Our Revolution backed Rep. Sara Innamorato in her run for PA House in 2018 alongside now-US Rep. Summer Lee, and now we’re proud to endorse for Allegheny County Executive - a key seat in Pittsburgh! With the progressive movement at her back, she’s now leading the establishment favorite by double digits in the polls, but we’re taking nothing for granted. As State Rep, Sara led dozens of bills for affordable housing, higher wages, environmental protections, reproductive healthcare, and much more. As County Exec, she’d have the power to directly affect people’s lives — from county-wide housing to addressing human rights violations in the county jail to protecting abortion care. ★ ★ ★ VICTORY! With the support of the progressive movement, community organizers and San Antonio Council Members Teri Castillo (D-5) and Jalen McKee-Rodriguez (D-3) won their races for re-election! Teri and Jalen helped pass a $17.50 minimum wage in the city and worked with the community to secure permanent affordable housing measures that passed by one vote on the council. “We’re bringing more people into the process for solutions on housing, workers' rights, and more,” [ ]Teri told us. Also in Texas, Our Revolution-backed ballot measure Prop A passed in Austin, showing voters want to hold police accountable and deter the use of excessive force. ★ ★ ★ NEW ENDORSEMENT! Our Revolution is backing Aaron Regunberg for US Congress in a crowded race to replace outgoing David Cicilline in RI-1. "I’m proud to have earned the endorsement of Our Revolution, which was founded by Bernie Sanders and supports progressive leaders with track records of organizing broad coalitions to make a difference for everyday people,” Aaron told [ ]The Boston Globe. “That’s what I’ve done here in Rhode Island by helping secure passage of paid sick days, higher wages, harm reduction strategies, and new clean energy programs — and that's what I'll do in Congress.” ★ ★ ★ We have a chance to elect several great progressives in Virginia on Election Day June 20th! For the State Senate, Our Revolution is backing Ben Litchfield (SD-27), Heidi Drauschak (SD-35), and Elizabeth Guzman; and for the State House, we’re backing Rozia Henson (HD-19). ★ ★ ★ We have a chance to build out progressive power at the city and state level where it can directly affect lives. The best way to make an impact on these races from anywhere in the country is to sign up to make calls with our team of volunteers. [ ]PHONEBANK THIS WEEK! ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ On Monday of this week, Our Revolution NYPAN activists were arrested alongside Susan Sarandon and OFW President Saru Jayaraman at an action calling for New York to raise the minimum wage and end the subminimum wage for tipped workers. Our state bill there has 33 cosponsors and growing because of all of you and your support, so we look forward to seeing NY step up and do what’s right. A reminder, if you want to get involved with our [ ]Raise the Wage Team to organize state by state for better pay and an end to subminimum wages for tipped workers. ★ ★ ★ On Monday, Our Revolution will join another global day of action to Make Amazon Pay. This time, we’re demanding Amazon pay its writers as we support striking members of the Writers Guild of America! [ ]Get involved with our global coalition holding demonstrations and showing solidarity across the internet with the hashtags #MakeAmazonPay and #WGAStrike. This struggle highlights how Amazon’s business extends across the global economy and threatens working conditions everywhere. It can also teach us the power we can build when allies stand together along supply chains and across borders. ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ ★ Become a co-sponsor for our campaign to End Medical Debt, and Stand with Bernie and Rep. Jayapal in the fight for Medicare for All. Chip in $10 (or $5 recurring) and we'll send you these two new stickers with our gratitude. Members get both stickers with any donation of $5 or more. [ ]GET YOUR STICKERS ★ ★ ★ Congress and the White House have the power to act. Our Revolution will be mobilizing actions on the ground and inside the halls of power to: cancel medical debt, end price-gouging, guarantee the right to free care and stop predatory collection tactics. Sign up to plug into this important work and we’ll reach out with an invite for the first team meeting Wednesday night following the Medicare for All reintroduction rally with Bernie and Rep. Jayapal! [ ]JOIN US IN THIS FIGHT! ★ ★ ★ On Wednesday, we’ll join Bernie Sanders, Reps. Pramila Jayapal and Debbie Dingell, and healthcare justice leaders to reintroduce Medicare for All! If you’re near DC, come out and show your support for universal, single-payer health care in America. This is a chance to show the strength of our movement and the power of our demand. [ ]JOIN US IN DC ★ ★ ★ Join us next Monday, May 22nd for our LIVE National Organize-to-Win Broadcast with Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison to discuss his new book Break the Wheel: Ending the Cycle of Police. [ ]RSVP ★ ★ ★ [ ]WHEN WE ORGANIZE, WE WIN! ★ ★ ★ [ ]Facebook | [ ]Twitter | [ ]Instagram | [ ]Youtube | [ ]Website | [ ]Sign Up [ ]Our membership swag Our Revolution is the largest entirely grassroots funded progressive group in America. [ ]Help us defend democracy and take the fight directly to the establishment by committing to a recurring donation of just $5 or more a month! [ ]GIVE MONTHLY [ ]GIVE ONCE You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION PO Box 66208 WASHINGTON, DC, 20035 Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution’s general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.

It’s a question I get a lot!
| | | [ ](https://email.win.donaldjtrump.com/c/eJw8kUGPmzAQhX-NuYHssTFw4JCsG1WNtKp6afcUmfGQeIUNBVOU_vqKKOn1m6en92lsmAbf31tWHRnAzbuFATB5YABndfz1rs8_zyb_9uX-nhtdqm2_whsDSPeJ_kefLQUF64edViZzLWANiBm1QtdK1EJVTXZrO-R92ckGus42soOyJKoEOI4CrHNV5lvgIHkpSuBCCF2IGhQq3TkkVaGSTPHNx8KN0Q7uM81rmAocQza0t5Sm5THpxOC0EK4zFZuPM7k9sTP7h3IbaPZo88_Rx5T3a3Sz9YuP1xzHEHxKRAxOEQdVN9SDsiWRKBvkTJ7WFC6BnF8Dk-bpq3e4jOuMxKRBf7FPhjZM1l8jk-bl9LqMMVFMTJqGS81AX-Lg3V7Zf-0_lsPH2-EBHx-R5sf3v4Dm-LvO5tZNmx8Gv-iKKX7dF-xu_wIAAP__q-yNlg)

Friend,
I’m often asked:_“how do you put up with the relentless attacks?”_
The answer is very simple:_**I put up with all of the vicious attacks, because I truly love our country.**_
America is the greatest country in history, and it honestly makes me sick to see what the Radical Left has done to destroy it.
**So no matter how much the Left attacks me, I will never surrender our country to their tyranny.**
Every time they attack me – whether that’s an indictment, surveillance, or even an arrest – the Left has ONLY STRENGTHENED my resolve to save our great nation.
If we allow extortionists to manipulate and interfere in our elections, we will lose America for good.
So... to our many, many opponents who thought that my ARREST would finally be the “end of Trump,” I say **I’M NOT GOING ANYWHERE!_NOT NOW – AND NOT EVER._**
_I also know that if they can come after me, they could one day do it to you. And I will never let that happen!_

name: Josh Hawley, me (2)
subject: Re: I want to make sure you saw this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee
body:
| | | Peter,

I know I emailed you earlier today, but this is important.

_There are just hours to go until our mid-month deadline, and we still haven't met our goal._

clean_body: we've extended the deadline to activate your membership | | | Dear Fellow Patriot, 2023 is a critical year for America's future, as we have limited time to correct course and take back our country from the radical Left. That's why we've extended the deadline to activate your membership with The Heritage Foundation. You can use this secure link to activate your membership right now: By becoming a Heritage member, you will join the ranks of half a million card-carrying constitutionalists who believe in our mission to go on offense against the radical Left, and win. But we cannot do this without your help. By activating your membership today, you will ensure Heritage has the resources to advance conservative policy solutions across the country while providing aggressive and meaningful oversight and accountability to the Biden administration and leaders of the leftist regime. Join us today by clicking this secure link before the deadline: Thank you for your consideration. I hope to stand alongside you in the fight to defeat the radical Left - there is no time to waste! Best, Kevin D. Roberts, Ph.D. The Heritage Foundation P.S. We are counting on your support to continue winning policy battles in Washington and across the country. name: Kelly Hall, Executive Director
subject: We went on offense to protect reproductive rights and won
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Fairness Project The Fairness Project
body:
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63336033/406424151/765663662?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2I3ZWNlMy01ZGYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww2ZvYv8Zo3HI8dXxfjJ69bhF1rNuSm4e4dN7n2beBI=&emci=1b4b9660-42f3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=bcb7ece3-5df3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=561582) | Team, Right around this time last year, many of us were feeling hopeless. The Supreme Court’s draft decision in _Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization_ was leaked to Politico — effectively overturning _Roe v. Wade_ and nationwide access to abortion. Before this leak, we knew this was a possibility. It’s why months before the ruling, we gathered partners in Michigan to see if running a ballot measure to preserve abortion access was even possible. The polling looked good, and so from there, Reproductive Freedom For All was launched. _But when this leak was published, it strengthened our resolve to fight**even harder.**_ We knew Michigan wouldn’t be the only state where abortion access was threatened by this decision, so we worked with partners in Vermont and across the country to protect abortion access everywhere possible. And this past November, after months of grueling work, **voters in five states decisively chose to protect their reproductive rights at the ballot box.** These wins were huge — not only because they were the first abortion protection ballot measures in history, _but because these wins set the tone for the fight for reproductive freedom in this country. _ **Time and again since November, voters have proven that banning abortion is a losing position.** And despite 8 in 10 Americans wanting to see abortion remain protected, extremist officials across the country _are still_ trying to put their personal beliefs before the rights of the constituents they were elected to represent. **Now, 10 states remain where we could use ballot measures to protect abortion access for good** — some are even states where active bans are currently on the books. Believe me when I say we are doing _everything we can_ to bring voters in these states the opportunity to codify their reproductive freedom. **We’re gearing up for the fights ahead.[_Will you join me and the rest of our Fairness Project team with a donation today to help us prepare for our next slate of ballot measure campaigns to protect abortion access, friend?_](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63336034/406424152/1379426826?refcode=E21471-20230515-RoeLeakTB-SL-Other-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2I3ZWNlMy01ZGYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww2ZvYv8Zo3HI8dXxfjJ69bhF1rNuSm4e4dN7n2beBI=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to the Fairness Project will go through immediately:_ | **[$15](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63336035/406424153/1379426826?express_lane=true&refcode=E21471-20230515-RoeLeakTB-EG-Other-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2I3ZWNlMy01ZGYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww2ZvYv8Zo3HI8dXxfjJ69bhF1rNuSm4e4dN7n2beBI=)** | **[$25](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63336036/406424154/1379426826?express_lane=true&refcode=E21471-20230515-RoeLeakTB-EG-Other-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2I3ZWNlMy01ZGYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Ww2ZvYv8Zo3HI8dXxfjJ69bhF1rNuSm4e4dN7n2beBI=)**

clean_body: | | Team, Right around this time last year, many of us were feeling hopeless. The Supreme Court’s draft decision in Dobbs v. Jackson Women’s Health Organization was leaked to Politico — effectively overturning Roe v. Wade and nationwide access to abortion. Before this leak, we knew this was a possibility. It’s why months before the ruling, we gathered partners in Michigan to see if running a ballot measure to preserve abortion access was even possible. The polling looked good, and so from there, Reproductive Freedom For All was launched. But when this leak was published, it strengthened our resolve to fighteven harder. We knew Michigan wouldn’t be the only state where abortion access was threatened by this decision, so we worked with partners in Vermont and across the country to protect abortion access everywhere possible. And this past November, after months of grueling work, voters in five states decisively chose to protect their reproductive rights at the ballot box. These wins were huge — not only because they were the first abortion protection ballot measures in history, but because these wins set the tone for the fight for reproductive freedom in this country. Time and again since November, voters have proven that banning abortion is a losing position. And despite 8 in 10 Americans wanting to see abortion remain protected, extremist officials across the country are still trying to put their personal beliefs before the rights of the constituents they were elected to represent. Now, 10 states remain where we could use ballot measures to protect abortion access for good — some are even states where active bans are currently on the books. Believe me when I say we are doing everything we can to bring voters in these states the opportunity to codify their reproductive freedom. We’re gearing up for the fights ahead.= | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to the Fairness Project will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = We’re grateful to have you in this fight with us, friend. = Kelly Hall Executive Director Fairness Project Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won 31 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. Contributions will not necessarily be used to support activity in any one state. You can request to get only the most important emails from our team , or you can . I’m proud to come from the freest state in America, Fellow conservative.

With great Republican leaders across our state, we’ve been able to achieve countless wins for the people of the Sunshine State.

subject: Fox News: Debt ceiling fight: Republican Medicaid plan shows how to make work requirements work right
body:

---
[](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=5a8177673480434484d6ec758a6c5528&_e=dHd0KKBfSFGxYkrxLAkmZxbvVh1bmDZNu19SCBAaAOvf4jvVcHErvJ7KrzMELQ1cjF6jhswkT9sLo2kanmoSH44Yv_YaF4DqRgE8S8FmaC-pCHeEgcaG743ef2rtp_X7zjYKjCaXJ0gShQqZ1hrMC6TUTOV87_Rl3yCRZIQkkvZoVLHO5xLVPDvZUEZlLckt9RhXclFfXuU41fV-63k43w%3D%3D)

_**Debt ceiling fight: Republican Medicaid plan shows how to make work requirements work right**_ By Bobby Jindal and Newt Gingrich
---
_Republicans already proved work requirements are smart. Medicaid plan lowers debt, improves health_
---
The House GOP’s Limit, Save, Grow Act to raise the debt ceiling contains within it a vitally important reform — work requirements for able-bodied adults to qualify for Medicaid. While the Congressional Budget Office estimates the Medicaid work requirements in the bill will save $109 billion over the next 10 years, the money savings are not why the reform is so important. In fact, work requirements for welfare programs are vital for the health and wellbeing of those receiving benefits. The House GOP Medicaid work requirements build on the highly successful reforms to unemployment insurance and other welfare programs Republicans enacted in 1996. [_**FINISH READING ON FOXNEWS.COM**_](http://links.americanxt.com/u/click?_t=45ea7b9dcedb45198104e7ca02a78270&_m=5a8177673480434484d6ec758a6c5528&_e=dHd0KKBfSFGxYkrxLAkmZ_jVgUs9li48ALs9RzAG7l8V60Jk6mzXST2pYM63LsVA4qEmftroFruViIeuhKsm2dz1ftfcFP14cJPNz5vtQ6qUFKx47SfKP-ZBlltlXdOK9thySj1NzndQUF3fKtSodUsLXFTwafZR1HumqQswP0VGJf9cGDHjlB9Fbz9vT88L-Q4bXg9cno5z4UiDOejhXeJxKpTVhJZWo6X4SOSxkQgSeYuzgVEGNDP98AmStftkgb8eJOO5NI_DestToW2BmPaPpBi6xikbXiep0NoVT5kT99BReMW1BwUrnvXmv0y29P3r_3mswES5Cluaxf69lw%3D%3D)

My campaign just received some really promising polling, and I wanted you to be one of the first to know.
My opponent has made bad votes recently. He’s put his party’s interests over his constituent’s interests — throwing our veterans, working families, and seniors under the bus.
When voters were asked about these policies, they made it clear that they didn’t support Juan Ciscomani’s blatant partisanship. Less than a quarter of respondents said they approved of his job performance.
This is a good sign, but we need to make sure we have the resources to tell all of AZ-06 how Ciscomani is failing them. Will you chip in now to support us? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/engel2024_foundingdonor?refcode=X_051523_Polling]

name: Team Helen
subject: Hey, can you call Philly voters to help them vote?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Helen Gym
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hhg-april23-em [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hhg-april23-em]
Hi team: Voting in Philadelphia ends tomorrow, and polling shows a virtual tie. What you do in the next 24 hours can really make all the difference in Philly’s future.

Will you sign up to call Philadelphia voters from home? [https://www.mobilize.us/helengym2023/event/562548/]

VIRTUAL VOTER TURNOUT PHONEBANK
Hosted by PA Working Families Party
Monday, May 15: 5-8 PM (join any time)
Tuesday, May 16: 9 AM - 12 PM (join any time)
SIGN UP NOW → [https://www.mobilize.us/helengym2023/event/562548]

By volunteering on Election Day or E-Day Eve, you’ll help us reach thousands of likely Helen supporters and ensure they have a plan to vote. Your call could give someone the motivation or support they need to get out and vote!

It does not matter if you’ve never volunteered before. We will provide the tools, training, and scripts you need to make an impact.

RSVP here to help Philadelphians vote. [https://www.mobilize.us/helengym2023/event/562548/]

Thank you,
Team Helen
Helen Gym is a Philly mom, a school teacher, a community organizer, and former City Councilmember. She has been fighting for Philly families and communities for decades — winning more resources for our schools, safer communities, and a more affordable Philadelphia. Our efforts are supported by everyday people like you who give what they can, whenever they can, because they want to see a safer, more prosperous, equitable Philadelphia that works for all of us. If you’d like to donate to Helen and help elect her as Philadelphia’s next Mayor, click here:
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hhg-em-feb2023]
Friends of Helen Gym
PO Box 11766
Philadelphia, PA 19101
United States
www.helengym.com [https://www.helengym.com/] | info@helengym.com [info@helengym.com]
Paid for by Friends of Helen Gym

name: Jon Tester
subject: seeding peas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by UNITEDemocrats PAC
body:
| Hey Peter,

I was back on the farm this past weekend with Sharla, and we spent the days seeding peas and pulling 16-hour days in the fields.

I love being a farmer. I knew I wanted to take over my family’s farm since I was eight years old. It gets my hands dirty and keeps me grounded. And reminds me why I fight so hard for Montana’s families.

But it also means I have a lot less time to fundraise than all of my colleagues. And trust me, I hate asking for money. I know how hard you work for every dollar—plus I’d rather spend my time out on the tractor anyway.

**The truth is, this race will be the most expensive Senate election in Montana’s history. Outside groups will pour upwards of $100 million into our state to distort my record and mislead voters.**

All this to say, we really do need money to combat attacks like that. So, here’s the part where I ask:

[**May is off to a way slower start than we predicted, and we’re at risk of falling short of our weekly goal. Could pitch in a split donation of any amount to get us back on track? By now, I’m sure you’ve already been made aware: **We were outraised and outspent last quarter by two other candidates in this race.** And on top of that, political experts are already predicting this could be the most expensive Senate primary in our nation's history.

But I'm not worried. I don't need to raise the most money in this race – I just need to raise enough to spread my progressive message across the state and win. Because with recent polling showing that a large number of California voters are undecided on who to vote for, **victory is well within our reach.**

That is why tonight’s midnight fundraising deadline is so important. These next few months will be absolutely critical to the success of this campaign, and I need to make sure I have the resources needed to reach voters and take on any new challenges I may face on the road to Election Day.

Believe me, I know this team is already working incredibly hard on this race, and with your help we raised nearly a million dollars last month alone.**I’m incredibly grateful for your support and everything you've done thus far, Peter. But with just hours until my midnight fundraising deadline, I'm falling short of my $25,000 goal.**

**That's why I'm personally asking you to join me and dig just a little bit deeper. If you’re able to, please:** [Will you split a donation – anything you can spare before midnight – between myself and The Collective PAC to help us hit this crucial midnight goal and build a progressive future for all? ](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wcVmDVfjroFVbol-C41V4cmHjMvWOD5NwNTo6pkCi84PKbUWnIJPgDzETGlz4pfffTJhyoks_nevsUhrl4u_5fPrvHYVuWaMfwzJ4xJjX59xT3AY2EYjWbbISqEAKv5H_TZK8MxFdt0y9DHbKv8r2QL8Gwai9dWWfM-h0G74G5CbeTkA7cs3UCeKsyKVP0dnT0GZGQIbst4DtCbUrpdcWvzWJPjkRmnMjLT9u3nMfBgdMIUyCU3aN-gUvXWnuoiyDYptnlc4R4UTphk6BBVmlN75xk3a2-rt1-w44z82bXdzBA7AFcXVgr_dTU-7zSkId8puGsLyx5Zr9GTgLuUX0VGse2aTgj0KQ1j9uGUIZAJQlv7xGuCrtVE26HwgG6ChL1F24Xv168PoHD6bf5cBfV/3w7/Iasfn0QySFKhvHVx5dCg_Q/h3/bcAVP2cS6VLrOIF9Mi16nNgsJXrVR_7jRhpHES9ykwQ) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Barbara Lee and The Collective PAC.

clean_body: | | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe | | Peter, sorry this email is a bit longer, but it's a very important update. By now, I’m sure you’ve already been made aware: We were outraised and outspent last quarter by two other candidates in this race. And on top of that, political experts are already predicting this could be the most expensive Senate primary in our nation's history. But I'm not worried. I don't need to raise the most money in this race – I just need to raise enough to spread my progressive message across the state and win. Because with recent polling showing that a large number of California voters are undecided on who to vote for, victory is well within our reach. That is why tonight’s midnight fundraising deadline is so important. These next few months will be absolutely critical to the success of this campaign, and I need to make sure I have the resources needed to reach voters and take on any new challenges I may face on the road to Election Day. Believe me, I know this team is already working incredibly hard on this race, and with your help we raised nearly a million dollars last month alone.I’m incredibly grateful for your support and everything you've done thus far, Peter. But with just hours until my midnight fundraising deadline, I'm falling short of my $25,000 goal. That's why I'm personally asking you to join me and dig just a little bit deeper. If you’re able to, please: | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately and split between Barbara Lee and The Collective PAC. | | | | | | Thank you for your support, Barbara Lee The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 110 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people. To Donate by Check: PO Box 15320, DC 20003 Paid for by the Collective PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. ** | | Sent via . [**May 15, 1886:** American poet and literary icon, Emily Dickinson, died in her hometown of Amherst, Massachusetts. After her death, her family found her collection of hand-sewn poetry booklets and proceeded to publish her entire collection posthumously. Her world-renowned collection makes Dickinson synonymous with American Literature.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBNVQ9_hgvg0P46eNdvz3UUCv8i4QnudTGbIkkN6PB0ASaGhDTJ4rE1hKipEp4T2aNH7Cb_jhj8yD4H44gjYBPmQZvBCdqlfiNXvsOmbLsaifRmjGU0-WbaP529C9HgsKlXR7e1AaPk20SkWlEFqR2snrtx9ieICe8m8Kk9oy6WbdC8YvhzEGnXD3J4AopObSA00oDIS5puEp0wjhHaCn-EaAEBZSw9BgektUo1XaWvk-) **Source -[poetryfoundation.org](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBNVQ9_hgvg0P46eNdvz3UUCv8i4QnudTGbIkkN6PB0ASaGhDTJ4rE1hKipEp4T2aNNvFzji7tHPNbBxqEA8LsZ1ypKay4OVeBbbbkGsxEWl-RKLmqVrkt9nEwsFMCFuFMaKR7lYXOlJm_EmeUimxso5mozpoKrNKyUAYrzrSZ3jcRl8e1ZfGGnmYBR0VmVXYww3uoK_9_q6VS9y_ggjbj7VC5eU55lMgeHDEZphGpIDb)[](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBIIUwme1rlvE1n3kNqChx5pU2ea-hw5eXpA76D0YiNfYvxDgjz3BghYw9_wPHq4OXsaNkUerVONKOWg5giTn5-kRRoKTN8dugTJgMkn3x9Y__-Q8a0UReAqDF8LOlAJ4xWwpVI64IbO3VykWtEvEiGuNxo62i1_uooz8Q6S3F2t87JQrPE2vIQDt_YpN02LLn7FnrYVPYEEdoFNWyAdXFifW_nQpQv8EMNJB6AujMS9s)**

[**May 15, 1928:** Disney’s iconic character, Mickey Mouse, made his debut in the silent film _Plane Crazy_. The theater audience saw a test screening with hopes that a distributor would pick up the film, but that failed to happen. Later in the year, Mickey Mouse in _Steamboat Willie_ was a major success, and _Plane Crazy_ was re-released with audio in 1929.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBCrmGN8Qtih6NCdHb1jLocFuZ0PxQif3YZXGLSv-OMNAsAbCo6fuidEhJp5VZYpELbM5Lv-Fy-No3bWEXyqKHss5Z5HTugYqYSPbBttrsYN5ach9Encf_SQ2igUwsciDI4cKhcVZ_SsdkjVEkKu7XbGJs9s29vW4mhpeBkRsnvXpeDTJqEgOMu8sDK77KTJVr2hrqIcZ-orwxerkUi3Mm30%3D) **Source - [wikipedia.org](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBCrmGN8Qtih6NCdHb1jLocFuZ0PxQif3YZXGLSv-OMNAsAbCo6fuidEhJp5VZYpELc-nvveVXmpnuYDTmHRE_GOWhTF4JVrRK1CGFtgMdqBV8tbuh_TRRaU96jLvppBtuTrxKwe38HaNYP7rEhq8QWjSzBnyvaZU9cmOuoab7H6untCHcQbQC8OTcI5BIH4OPqy36tPEh9cW-ZaMS3kHVBw%3D)**

[**May 15, 1969:** Justice Abe Fortas resigned from the Supreme Court of the United States under threat of impeachment for financial violations. Appointed to the court by President Lyndon B. Johnson, Fortas was also nominated by him to become Chief Justice, but he requested to have his name withdrawn from consideration and ultimately resigned after public scrutiny and allegations.](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBBzPEKMuKZOv84rMt5L-iGAeAzjtBL9APeR9zB-eEl7HDGqteVMWbeOowuQ-4uDe7wifoBpb24PCAth2BbazEO3YTrbRXt-cVOHwAz7wZTCO_wwvmIMDW7vhit1v7iOyJcAOvnjByEUKS-qeBm5um2aXsjyUXDojLx416XUMYQ673vPbg9N2W4DXOnOhh2bzQmNpMhpGRzk3x0iBd7wowls%3D) **Source -[britannica.com](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=e72a5dd1fe324a15b2384ce63fafcc45&_e=nb_u7Oy7Gg2PuIzDUJlaBBzPEKMuKZOv84rMt5L-iGAeAzjtBL9APeR9zB-eEl7HDGqteVMWbeOowuQ-4uDe7_EzSGcpa1VSmvjiWEljEFjt7mLf8HIZlR1siB3ihmAEhtGhOdcIcdJXuF3IMgI61xDA2FyNYbI1B646Z7ENik9xwJEQe83UXsKK_I_B40viRNTRlsVfYHMuWhPktr_LMU98PY-KEQMeFdPIorQplMw%3D)**

Peter,
Oregon is a state of so much natural beauty and such wonderful people – I’m so honored to represent its 5th District.

But my heart is breaking for our citizens, because every time they leave their houses, this is what they see:

[](https://u32713810.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=P2XsNzd-2BU60NKIIDkRBMXuvnoTrkCmF6-2Fx8aZdCr3SNXTpOlLQlhNvp5b68pSwdV3SXBnM6XRx6vPl8QKxJ1pu-2BE99Rts9hMjQFXAktQtw5Lv-2BUmw-2Btb73ybpLWEO7zBMHsQs9KJbjZGFej9oz84RXFs6IaoiPz4EcDe7CCuMUlsZrfe3-2BSw8s6JGqGq24d-2FmeFmNSZbbGhIm2Pv39-2BUNiboQo8sCOzIB69ZW8HCho1LAW-2BfXBHWJft21TTxBnTn4pUu_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymhuTI2jG3IYR5NWbV52qgmdkLMmWeLnwK0KfPGgXzXHvoIGcfoQrAuI0Zkzwvv9dkHghc1ZFPh4s2VPIB-2BUJPR1ce1MwXOgEsQtpD9Vs-2BDtcw-2Fj-2FpBehS-2Fh73ApbgPoaUT9pTD0gi-2B14XbcMWTd2ANsQFkAyMWTRJUMRcyGqwJjF56mRzZaYpZJ-2F4LoN4cNwMF1fIOwL1iOPfxCWGh9xnCsr0oUglq3RDb-2FXR3rPrzqXAidOhhmw7CV1FOblkPKbJzdkCB9M-2BMhqIXlp0NJ9BwCChlnc8y0TCDBfKy-2Bqc3w7dEqMZaL6RBhkc-2Fl3PULt59qrTk57L-2F3z7DF4pDMgvAsLTcblFOj1a4q7CEqaR19PZ3auPKZfC2aHZA-2Bq7qwxAkbqPxWpChaWzsMLnWVIelqaSXK6M5uA67yoSzK9olxVlEoIl7F4IpUI-2BjaNZ-2BFUEhiQYJiOlT9ZiKjm5H6Bm0NEodtkoQJS6mmsW2s2Qs6Ac2o0DEixhyiEG9Bk4-2BN-2F0EaUXoWj0xVEjTdzDpvneQjNueTa47e9z8MR35-2F1HM3W-2B4lAFM4fzxwgEVfJCtGhDuziIAqwAlxR27-2BLvtEK5mCewKgSydH2rjXv9HFQmEIcPuLVqST-2BP-2F1S5ofq2keKOWEGDTj3HrQWOIIGwq8RvxrtPN5Y5oSIV-2FWyrGGeddxN1QNl9VTbKrs-2F2sjCUS43FhKEIXQbbNA7Yic3j84JqPEcnAYLC5TWUvj5GGuuqp4YdGx1Tgg8R5zn-2FNGFRr-2BD6ui0pOyTVvHUPcKkR6OlyNRVPGNU0-2FAcTXspJQ-2BgZIZNxNPmZ3i0ye94CBwarxoGJhcWStiKDEC0IWjvBwyeO1F2eHU2TFus24Qnd6-2FK3VKD8QrFekL2qcj7OQ3fe9eEziEMjonKKKcww3C1D6IZ1QWmHfBPqhh1nyTansb1qUs7pXrpBgn-2Ba3owkWxcz9IW0XL4-2Fc-2Bk6GthgdkloV1By3fFACLkB1D3meY0yr3Gc-2Ft-2Bw-3D-3D)

We have a homelessness problem, we have a drug problem, and we have a crime problem – and you, Peter, deserve better than that.

__It’s my mission as a Congresswoman to give you better than that.
__
But being effective as a Congresswoman all starts with having a rock-solid team standing behind me, and today I’m personally inviting you to join that team, Peter, as a 2023 Sustaining Member.

Just think, 14,000 Oregonians are homeless on any given night.

Our police budget has been slashed by $26 MILLION.

Homicides are up 800%.

This is not good enough for the people of Oregon, and it’s high time our leaders address these issues head-on.

That’s what I ran for Congress to do, and that’s what I’m fighting every day to do, but if I’m going to succeed, I need your help.

clean_body: | | | | This is an invaluable opportunity | Peter, Oregon is a state of so much natural beauty and such wonderful people – I’m so honored to represent its 5th District. But my heart is breaking for our citizens, because every time they leave their houses, this is what they see: We have a homelessness problem, we have a drug problem, and we have a crime problem – and you, Peter, deserve better than that. It’s my mission as a Congresswoman to give you better than that. But being effective as a Congresswoman all starts with having a rock-solid team standing behind me, and today I’m personally inviting you to join that team, Peter, as a 2023 Sustaining Member. | | | | | Just think, 14,000 Oregonians are homeless on any given night. Our police budget has been slashed by $26 MILLION. Homicides are up 800%. This is not good enough for the people of Oregon, and it’s high time our leaders address these issues head-on. That’s what I ran for Congress to do, and that’s what I’m fighting every day to do, but if I’m going to succeed, I need your help. I know that with you on my team, we can make Oregon a better home for all of us. Lori Chavez-DeRemer Congresswoman OR-05 | | | | | | | | | Even as a Congresswoman, Lori’s most important title is mom. That’s how she knows the issues your family faces, and that’s why she fights everyday to make life better for every Oregon family, and every American. Aside from that, all you need to know are the issues she’s fighting for in Congress: If that sounds like the kind of agenda you can get behind, If you’d prefer to give by check, please use the below address: Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Congress 13203 SE 172nd Ave Suite 166 #399 Happy Valley, OR 97086 Forward This message to FIVE FRIENDS AND FAMILY to spread the word on how to achieve a better future for Oregon and America → ** Also text “LORI” to 71800 or click here to receive all of Lori’s messages | | | | Lori Chavez-DeRemer 13203 SE 172nd Ave, Suite 166 #399, Happy Valley, OR 97086 ==

I believe she reached out recently to introduce herself. Now I wanted to take a moment to tell you a bit more about Madeleine's strong leadership.

In Congress, Mad is a serious, solutions-oriented legislator committed to addressing the needs and challenges of hardworking folks across both PA-04 and the nation. You don’t have to take my word for it, though – her record speaks for itself, she played a major role in:
* Lowering healthcare costs for seniors

* Historic investments in infrastructure to rebuild our roads, bridges, rails, and more – all while creating thousands of new jobs

* Passing once-in-a-generation investments in green energy solutions

* Voting to codify reproductive rights once and for all

**Mad is not just talking the talk – she’s walking the walk.** But while Mad is hard at work, her colleagues across the aisle continue to prioritize dysfunction over delivering for the American people.

You don’t have to take my word for it, though – her record speaks for itself, she played a major role in: Lowering healthcare costs for seniors Historic investments in infrastructure to rebuild our roads, bridges, rails, and more – all while creating thousands of new jobs Passing once-in-a-generation investments in green energy solutions Voting to codify reproductive rights once and for all Mad is not just talking the talk – she’s walking the walk. body:
I really hope I get the chance to speak to you personally!
# [](https://act.lateefahsimon.com/go/39?t=1&akid=27%2E1163%2EC3QTnl)
This could be us:
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230515-1?refcode=em20230515-1&refcode2=27_1163_C3QTnl&akid=27%2E1163%2EC3QTnl)
**No seriously, I want to personally give you a call!** The backbone of our campaign for Congress in California's 12th district is thousands of everyday people like **you** supporting our vision for a more just, inclusive, and progressive district.
I want to thank the grassroots supporters like you who are powering our movement by giving some of you a personal phone call to express my gratitude.
**[So let's talk! 📞 Donate $5 or anything you can today and you could receive a phone call from me!](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230515-1?refcode=em20230515-1&refcode2=27_1163_C3QTnl&akid=27%2E1163%2EC3QTnl)**
_(Make sure you have your phone number saved in your ActBlue information so I can give you a call at the right number!)_
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230515-1?refcode=em20230515-1&refcode2=27_1163_C3QTnl&akid=27%2E1163%2EC3QTnl)**
I don't take it for granted for a second how much your support means to me. You're truly a part of this team. Your support allows me to keep fighting for our beloved community -- and you know I'll be bringing each and every one of our voices with me to Congress!
**[Please allow me to show my gratitude by talking to as many of our supporters as possible. Donate anything you can right now and you could be one of the passionate supporters that I call.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ls-em20230515-1?refcode=em20230515-1&refcode2=27_1163_C3QTnl&akid=27%2E1163%2EC3QTnl)**

Thank you for your support, today and every day,
Lateefah Simon

Katie Porter for California

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63338125/406431539/-30252776?sourceid=1050057&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWM2MDc4MC02NWYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ExZB6zb4sBf9etNC2KBpzqBbjUyJVsIVHaRr3KuVKrg=&emci=a2c60780-65f3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=a9c60780-65f3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=5177934) **Polling via Pew Research Center:** 6 in 10 Americans say abortion should be legal.

_We're running our own poll to send a clear message to the GOP that the vast majority of Americans support abortion rights. We're still missing your input, Peter._ [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/63338126/406431540/-30252776?sourceid=1050057&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOWM2MDc4MC02NWYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ExZB6zb4sBf9etNC2KBpzqBbjUyJVsIVHaRr3KuVKrg=&emci=a2c60780-65f3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=a9c60780-65f3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=5177934) This should be obvious, Peter: No politician should be able to make personal reproductive care decisions for us.

In a direct attack on our constitutional right to privacy, 19 states have banned nearly all abortion. North Carolina, North Dakota, and Florida have advanced bans in just the past few weeks. And, an appeals court is now considering a case that could ban mifepristone — a medication used in a majority of abortions — nationwide.

We're fighting back. **We refuse** to stand idly by as people are denied potentially lifesaving health care. **We refuse** to stay silent as doctors are arrested for providing basic reproductive care. **And, we refuse** to let GOP politicians make decisions about when and how we have a family.

**That's why we're launching this live poll to show the GOP that most Americans are pro-choice. You've been specifically selected to represent Pennsylvania in our flash poll, and we need your response. name: Al Franken via Blue Amp Action
subject: Together Josh and I did good stuff when he was my counsel on the U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee – good stuff like protecting victims of domestic violence. Will you help us win one of the most important races of 2024, so he can do more good for all of us?
body:
We can take this House seat
| | | | |
---
| | |
---
| | | You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[ unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb4acdb09-ae6b-4b20-a2b0-cb7de9937bbf/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) _._
---
|
---
| |
---
| | | | | [__](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6e6dfd12-726f-425d-be16-9cd3e2ca1b38/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Hey, it’s Al Franken. [I’m writing because I want you to split $10 or whatever you can (or more than you can) between Blue Amp Action and my friend Josh Riley to help him win one of the most important races in the country in 2024.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tcbb8c07d-7d49-4da5-9447-1e4da3a23998/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Here’s why I support Josh — and why you should too! Josh knows policy issues backward and forward. Together Josh and I did good stuff when he was my counsel on the U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee – good stuff like protecting victims of domestic violence. In the Violence Against Women Act, Josh and I wrote a provision that protected survivors of domestic violence from homelessness. And Josh and I got that protection through a divided Senate, a Republican House, and signed into law. Republicans lie and say Josh was bad on crime, but here’s the truth. Josh and I advanced legislation that funded the police! Providing bullet-proof vests for local and state police. And funding crisis intervention training to teach police officers how to de-escalate situations. [Will you join me by splitting a contribution between Blue Amp Action and Josh’s campaign today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td2b306ea-ea36-4183-8938-11d4545c30d1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T605e9188-fd0a-459f-8d8d-24703a4a5bea/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| Together in the Senate, we also made a lot of Republican witnesses look very bad at hearings when we cross-examined them. Josh really, really cares about all the stuff I care about. And he is the hardest worker I know. And finally, this tossup seat is considered one of the most competitive in the nation. [So, what do you say? Will you join me by splitting a contribution between Blue Amp Action and Josh’s campaign today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf219a7e4-6354-4225-bf59-018b12b0ec61/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) This is important to me. And to our nation. Thanks, Al [P.S. I always add a P.S to these letters to make another ask. It’s a cheap tactic but for some reason it works! DONATE HERE.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T89ff354d-f223-4bf7-bcd1-d898400633c9/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | |
---
|
---
| | | | | | [**DONATE NOW**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T7385b3f3-4407-41c4-8a4d-7b43889ca801/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | Cliff Schecter President, Blue Amp Action Amplify Good
---|---
| | | About BlueAmp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country.
---
|
---

---|---

1776 Mentor Ave. #411 | Norwood, Ohio 45212
513.438.0171 | info@blueampaction.com
---

The Deep State HATED the idea of a political outsider leading the White House. They knew they could never control me or bully me into submission. So, they tried to frame me as a Russian agent – despite having ZERO EVIDENCE – in an attempt to destroy me and stop the one movement that puts YOU first. As I’ve always said, they’re not really after me. They’re after YOU, I’m just standing in their way. And you better believe that I will keep standing in their way, because I will NEVER, EVER SURRENDER the greatest country in history. 2024 is our final chance to save our Republic. Either we destroy the Deep State, or the Deep State will destroy America. | Thank you, | Donald J. Trump 45th President of the United States Contributions to Trump Save America JFC are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. name: Cori Bush HQ
subject: Politician + activist = ‘politivist’
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
We need more leaders like Cori who have the courage to listen to others and push boundaries.
#
Peter,
Since Cori's election to the People's House, she has quickly set herself apart from many of her colleagues.
As a nurse, pastor, mom, Movement for Black Lives activist, and now a politivist, Cori hasn’t taken off her activist hat since arriving. It takes a strong leader to walk the tightrope Cori does and be able to do the organizing on the outside then come inside and negotiate for the transformative policies our communities need.
Every day, Cori brings her lived experiences to Congress and works 110% to advocate for the millions of folks who are living the same experiences. These lived experiences are at the heart of our mission in Congress to transform and save lives.
We need more leaders like Cori who have the courage to listen to others and push boundaries. Over her career, Cori has been on the ground and with the same people she was elected to represent and uses this to inform the bold legislation that will answer some of the most pressing issues.
[**But this hasn't stopped our GOP opponents from looking to disrupt all of the progress we've made so far. To keep Cori in Congress fighting for us, will you chip in to our people-powered movement today?**](https://act.coribush.org/go/238?c0=cori-em-20230515.ECMrzp&refcode=em20230515-2697&t=1&akid=2697%2E1082990%2EmWp8Zd)

name: Becca Balint
subject: What default would mean for Vermont:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont
body:
47,000 Vermonters at risk of losing Medicaid coverage.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63339560/406435722/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YzJiMzc0Ni02YmYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DdLfxOjGD8dYazX6ShX3uSAM9MXZxE5snUhgFmzoHTI=&emci=5152d634-2ef3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=7c2b3746-6bf3-ed11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=43402272) | Peter, If you’ve seen the news, then you’ve probably heard a lot about the debt ceiling situation. _I wanted to share what all of this means, and how it would impact Vermonters._ Raising the debt limit simply means the government would be able to pay the bills it has already accrued and continue providing services like health care, Social Security payments, and food stamps to working class families. **Congressional Republicans’ Default on America Act, their so-called solution to the debt ceiling situation, demands that heartless and _massive_ cuts be made to key government programs in exchange for raising the debt limit. Here’s a snapshot of the impact this bill would have:**
* 47,000 Vermonters would be at risk of losing Medicaid coverage.
* At least 700 Vermont kids would lose preschool or child care.
* Health benefits for 266,000 Vermonters would be put at risk.
* 4,000 Vermont families would lose access to nutrition food assistance programs.
* 10 rail safety inspection days would be lost for our public transportation.

clean_body: 47,000 Vermonters at risk of losing Medicaid coverage. | | Peter, If you’ve seen the news, then you’ve probably heard a lot about the debt ceiling situation. I wanted to share what all of this means, and how it would impact Vermonters. Raising the debt limit simply means the government would be able to pay the bills it has already accrued and continue providing services like health care, Social Security payments, and food stamps to working class families. Congressional Republicans’ Default on America Act, their so-called solution to the debt ceiling situation, demands that heartless and massive cuts be made to key government programs in exchange for raising the debt limit. Here’s a snapshot of the impact this bill would have: 47,000 Vermonters would be at risk of losing Medicaid coverage. At least 700 Vermont kids would lose preschool or child care. Health benefits for 266,000 Vermonters would be put at risk. 4,000 Vermont families would lose access to nutrition food assistance programs. 10 rail safety inspection days would be lost for our public transportation. I'm fighting to make sure America pays its bills and continues to invest in the priorities and programs that Vermonters rely on.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Republicans are ready to drive America off of an economic cliff for an act of political showmanship. After all, Republicans voted three separate times to raise the debt ceiling under President Trump, and America has never in history defaulted on its debt. The GOP’s attempt to force America into default will have real consequences for Vermont families. subject: Setting the narrative for 2024

body:
Friend, we're only a few hours away from our mid-quarter deadline. This is a critical benchmark as we work to keep pace for a strong public fundraising result this summer — one that will help set the narrative for how the rest of our race unfolds.

[**I'm reaching out to ask personally for your help, because we're still short by $671 of where we need to be. Can you rush $10 before midnight to help us make up the gap?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63340197/406437907/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230515MM2_MM_JW_FULL_5_NG_N_L1&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTA3OTE4OC02Y2YzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m_fbctXxEVGCEcFEzmWCcuDyqPDCQKxXPRqzV-2btFY=)

Republican opponents could announce against me at any time, and the pace of the election cycle will quickly pick up before we know it. If we miss our early goals, the GOP will be able to smear me and gain ground in a seat that Democrats _have_ to hold if we want to win back the House.

I'm working to make sure voters know the story of the policies I voted to pass – from expanding broadband access, to delivering relief that has set our economy back on track, to taking on big special interests so we could lower the cost of prescription drugs for families. I'm proud of this record, and I'll fight every day to defend it and work to build upon it.

**It's through the support of grassroots donors like you that we are able to get our message out. So if you can, please donate $10 before midnight by clicking here:[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jw_em](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63340198/406437908/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230515MM2_MM_JW_FULL_5_NG_N_L1&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmYTA3OTE4OC02Y2YzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m_fbctXxEVGCEcFEzmWCcuDyqPDCQKxXPRqzV-2btFY=)**

Thank you so much,

Jennifer

Sterling, VA 20165

_**2023 is a major year for Virginia elections.**_

Every seat in our General Assembly is up for re-election, and Virginia Democrats will make clear that our policies are good for people, communities, and our economy, and that our legislators – state and federal – work hard for the neighbors we represent.

_**That’s why this year Abigail launched the Securing Progress Across our Nation PAC (SPAN PAC)**_ to support candidates across the state and nation to help build strength for critical elections.

clean_body: Abigail launched the Securing Progress Across our Nation PAC (SPAN PAC) to support candidates across the state and nation to help build strength for critical elections. | = | Peter, 2023 is a major year for Virginia elections. Every seat in our General Assembly is up for re-election, and Virginia Democrats will make clear that our policies are good for people, communities, and our economy, and that our legislators – state and federal – work hard for the neighbors we represent. That’s why this year Abigail launched the Securing Progress Across our Nation PAC (SPAN PAC) to support candidates across the state and nation to help build strength for critical elections. And so far, the PAC has been supporting amazing candidates across the Commonwealth! | | | [](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=bf272a30ed474ca89c41c9a13a5827ec&_e=GJhS9nxP8wWg2y7xdXXNClVhrwrMjTACifa9v_p12giSBOiFIVBIMjvJyOdi8-Q0CAGRzdJsoHNJnihySw83h_BLH-pVo4JC76GWm2yBpDVUQvm-BrzNEBT4oUTTcZfXbegteyYFJMxKnbk8fYAeNpe40DrNGFzAVxnqTZagvq2jWat4_0QgPvZDUYpBi-GvzTHYINR1hQv7IJYN8sGK65ejPJt4TxovTOZFbQKmbz-YPZXoxj6FUotL7cmDHPPYvTK674kReiI6jfCwl8YV7HXdtjTUEQHsl3sF0pNQKBc%3D)

| | May 15, 2023
---
| WEEKLY NEWSLETTER
---
| Issue 11
---
| | **A Note from CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET)** I wrote a column for Townhall titled "It Didn't Have to End This Way, " which discusses the border crisis under President Biden and the dangers children face relying on violent cartels to enter the US illegally. When POLARIS National Security tried to share the column on Facebook, the [platform deemed it "hate speech"](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=bf272a30ed474ca89c41c9a13a5827ec&_e=GJhS9nxP8wWg2y7xdXXNCpFoW2f0YiIjDaBNLPeGw_FQ8kjh4dEDtmeXim7E-fx5bHSbbELVJqBVvPB5vYkIVlBVKRsZKN09IuRX9kMhDxxBtoM9ydtgEtdqH5K0nee_Bym-llBtlF3RRhQpGUT7q51QrhYzjkfodb6aoYKypxxTIhOzl4De4X-GMeBYtffrlsPtD50htOcQyErnPUKoh97MaF5oRpgcmDvFj7pou5qvd4Ft2CR6tV-Xkvsw65-MCURPFO4IxK8NQdCrTW5i7dLP95T4lqKQLLRdPh-mMC_niC9f4KtlYtmpGeelLL8OUPpeXJ1XP4larY3f1QqvH9zK-_PYTLZDu58hCaS5OXFRUxSDi4WpGawtIY2hOgMV) and against community standards. What about telling the truth about the border and addressing the plight of migrant children would be considered hateful? This suppression by Big Tech is due to its uncomfortable truths about the Biden administration's handling of the border. The incident is just one in a pattern of Big Tech suppressing Conservative voices that challenge the narrative promoted by the Democrats. My op-ed is below, and see if you can figure out why Facebook considers it too dangerous for public consumption. That’s not the only attack from Democrats I’ve experienced in the last week. Disturbing revelations recently emerged that the left-wing activists with very close ties to elected Democrat politicians plotted to secretly destroy the lives and careers of conservatives and Christians who have spoken out at local school board meetings or for greater parental rights. These Democrats made a “hit list.” I am number thirty-one on their list. My family and I have been on hit lists before when we escaped communist Vietnam. Never did I think something like this would happen in America. The fight for common-sense and basic civility has never been more critical. Help Unleash America’s mission to restore confidence in American politics. \- CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET), Honorary Chairman of [Unleash America PAC](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=bf272a30ed474ca89c41c9a13a5827ec&_e=GJhS9nxP8wWg2y7xdXXNClVhrwrMjTACifa9v_p12giSBOiFIVBIMjvJyOdi8-Q0CAGRzdJsoHNJnihySw83h_BLH-pVo4JC76GWm2yBpDVUQvm-BrzNEBT4oUTTcZfXbegteyYFJMxKnbk8fYAeNoJRq3YMbnasAuH4GuVC5f6VgXEtuoxvCgcPFjdORLBHlxdNv4VxMpl7l9GAFeKDG46r91TeZzY3hOgM-6DsF6_jZ04VAum7Dcf2vqC5tZhy-cuYruy1JMuEgzynqgFP_WFJoBuk86ApigccQgcV5Kc%3D)
---
| | |
---
**It Didn't Have to End This Way**
---
[](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=bf272a30ed474ca89c41c9a13a5827ec&_e=GJhS9nxP8wWg2y7xdXXNCvRukGHBdAKtmjFvBBK3wPWs_xFiplTqZWjR9Blhr9g3LBrbAmswfoKX87gMm8cnH9cJ6d92kaehCYiZLMuicvOqcNheY_NqVttZb2gxYMHM75p14QrbZm4pbjJxQPEaB8My10ixdwivE72beCbuYaO_btwm3wKGRZUlwq46nruhCSbFOgYUCjLrCy8WakmaIv8NS9X20-5XBwIDPURycyb3RBH7cvO_fDHp2YyVtMuY02WZ9keAlh9bKz8GXz5iT89Sv6NWPZ3V74GNEizD1IPtBk3suh7n9XQVG4XGZvcZ0loS65UoZ4BoF1nWJ-VjXWdZE3xGdYvCGazZpFNDz6y0egbI6_aZyWxUjd04z7ig)

Cao shares a personal story of his mother sewing notes into their clothes as they fled Vietnam, highlighting the desperation of parents trying to protect their children. He argues that the Biden administration's policies have turned the American dream into a nightmare, emphasizing the importance of securing the border and respecting legal immigration. Cao encourages Americans to remember and hold Biden accountable at the ballot box.
---
|
---
**This Vietnam Human Rights Day, America must confront its own authoritarians**
---
[](http://links.unleashamericapac.co/u/click?_t=4fd2065bb87d4411bcd7523509144268&_m=bf272a30ed474ca89c41c9a13a5827ec&_e=GJhS9nxP8wWg2y7xdXXNChv17A9qXKX-9wB8avhMKK51H2EKb6gFshCKlsbeMLA_FqP8hJpCV8hp-OfhpOo_44aAHDgOR8HcTQAzJSU0zstGD0RoT4Gck1avToEbRcWQBODNGmmV4jiasf1y5UWL3fEfpnBe8yDv15KYaPWVy_7EUPXvkPzPE9o39GGk9QQwVoPlN5rjbEJiUMfii14UsX1ShsCCYrIW5kF0PMvwSro4BGqvIV6eoV55W1_ZzqTbkzjKBQxKLClaLj6SN31kLd8nSlA5msgr6ZLme79yHfPeBNU116LIgs6drxLldWR9SgAl7DMHECMZ4DZXfPPgQc2EqGo1ojxqVeHaI-p323gjsGXYUkVrczS-eWiVCV-Y3kpHO_fipNlGCALVG8Ug7mem0ddHSFS63OI_-bTWG3FriLvCKXwQDwwJfT0Dlnir)

---
As a Vietnamese American refugee, Cao highlights the significance of Vietnam Human Rights Day and the ongoing abuses of communism in an op-ed with the Washington Examiner. Sharing personal experiences of family members lost and the shadow cast over those who remain in Vietnam, Cao emphasizes the importance of representation in Congress for those who have escaped oppressive regimes. Calling for self-reflection and the election of leaders restoring confidence in American politics, he reaffirms his commitment to defending the U.S. as a beacon of hope.

clean_body: | | | | | May 15, 2023 | WEEKLY NEWSLETTER | Issue 11 | | A Note from CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET) I wrote a column for Townhall titled "It Didn't Have to End This Way, " which discusses the border crisis under President Biden and the dangers children face relying on violent cartels to enter the US illegally. When POLARIS National Security tried to share the column on Facebook, the and against community standards. What about telling the truth about the border and addressing the plight of migrant children would be considered hateful? This suppression by Big Tech is due to its uncomfortable truths about the Biden administration's handling of the border. The incident is just one in a pattern of Big Tech suppressing Conservative voices that challenge the narrative promoted by the Democrats. My op-ed is below, and see if you can figure out why Facebook considers it too dangerous for public consumption. That’s not the only attack from Democrats I’ve experienced in the last week. Disturbing revelations recently emerged that the left-wing activists with very close ties to elected Democrat politicians plotted to secretly destroy the lives and careers of conservatives and Christians who have spoken out at local school board meetings or for greater parental rights. These Democrats made a “hit list.” I am number thirty-one on their list. My family and I have been on hit lists before when we escaped communist Vietnam. Never did I think something like this would happen in America. The fight for common-sense and basic civility has never been more critical. Help Unleash America’s mission to restore confidence in American politics. \- CAPT Hung Cao USN (RET), Honorary Chairman of | | | It Didn't Have to End This Way Cao shares a personal story of his mother sewing notes into their clothes as they fled Vietnam, highlighting the desperation of parents trying to protect their children. He argues that the Biden administration's policies have turned the American dream into a nightmare, emphasizing the importance of securing the border and respecting legal immigration. Cao encourages Americans to remember and hold Biden accountable at the ballot box. | This Vietnam Human Rights Day, America must confront its own authoritarians As a Vietnamese American refugee, Cao highlights the significance of Vietnam Human Rights Day and the ongoing abuses of communism in an op-ed with the Washington Examiner. Sharing personal experiences of family members lost and the shadow cast over those who remain in Vietnam, Cao emphasizes the importance of representation in Congress for those who have escaped oppressive regimes. Calling for self-reflection and the election of leaders restoring confidence in American politics, he reaffirms his commitment to defending the U.S. as a beacon of hope. | Social Media to Share Here are a few of our top posts from social media this week. Be sure to like and share, and when you tweet at Hung remember to use #UnleashAmerica! | | | | | | That's it for this week, thank you for reading. Be sure to follow Unleash America on social media! | | | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Unleash America PAC. name: from Rep. Dana Criswell
subject: 2023 Legislative Disappointments
body:
The 2023 legislative session ended on April 1 with the passage of the final bill of the legislative session. At approximately 1:00 am the final vote was cast for the last bill of the session. Never mind that the extended deadline had been exceeded and at midnight of March 31 all appropriation bills were dead. The Mississippi legislature once again ignored the law, the constitution and the good of the people and passed a bill that appropriated millions of dollars to "member projects".

The 2023 legislative session ended on April 1 with the passage of the final bill of the legislative session. At approximately 1:00 am the final vote was cast for the last bill of the session. Never mind that the extended deadline had been exceeded and at midnight of March 31 all appropriation bills were dead. The Mississippi legislature once again ignored the law, the constitution and the good of the people and passed a bill that appropriated millions of dollars to "member projects".
Actually that was a fitting end to a disappointing year in the Mississippi legislature. The Mississippi legislature has been run by majority Republicans since 2012 and has had a super majority for the past 8 year. But even with a super majority of Republicans, Mississippi politicians have continued to spend record amounts of the people's money.
Thanks for reading Dana Criswell! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribe](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZGFuYWNyaXN3ZWxsLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTE4NjMzNjQ0LCJzIjoxNTI2NzgxLCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNjg0MTg5MDAxLCJleHAiOjE2ODY3ODEwMDEsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.FItK7f3NTgBn-VB59xMIEDlQrYs0Rw3Gb18ea1n4jLk?)
This year was no different. The final count was a budget of over $7.6 billion, the largest in our state's history. The state is ending the 2022 fiscal year with over a $1 billion surplus. That means the state of Mississippi and it's Republican lead government has taken from the people of this state over a billion dollars more than it needed.
But, even with a billion dollar surplus Republicans in the legislature refused to return any of the money, opting instead to spend every dime of your hard earned money on their pet projects to ensure their re-election.
Earlier in the session during a Republican Caucus meeting Speaker Gunn brought up the idea of eliminating the state income tax. With a billion dollar surplus it would have been an easy task and one most would expect a group of Republicans to embrace. But that was not the case. At least 12 Republicans stood and begged the Speaker to kill a bill that eliminated the income tax. They expressed concerns that were drawn directly from the Democrat Party play-book and ultimately killed any chance of returning money to the people.
One Republican actually stood up during the meeting and asked if he had somehow entered a Democrat party meeting.
But the disappointments didn't end there.
The majority Republican legislature continued its Democrat Party look-alike session by passing a bill that makes it difficult for Mississippians to purchase an Electric Vehicle. I'm not a huge fan of EVs but it is your decisions, not mine, if you want to buy a Tesla or an F450. But the Mississippi legislature decided to protect car dealerships my eliminating competition. Once again, you lose and the politicians gain campaign donations.
One good thing the legislature did was attempt to fix a 2022 law that required a contractor license to do any work on a home. This misguided 2022 legislation was sold as "consumer protection" but in reality it made it technically illegal for you to work on your parents home. The legislature attempted to fix its 2022 error this year by repealing a portion of the bad law, but our Governor, who seems to have also decided the Democrat way is the best way, vetoed that bill because the Contractor Licensing Board urged him to protect them instead of the citizens. Once again, you lose and the politicians gain campaign donations.
In a third strike our Governor, Lt. Governor, Speaker and a majority of Republicans decided to adopt the Democrats way of thinking and expanded medicaid. But wait, they say don't worry it was only a little bit! Medicaid expansion is not "free" money as the Democrats and their newly converted Republican friends call it. Medicaid is money from the federal government and a bunch of money from the state government. So is the federal money free? NO, it is money the federal government takes from you. So now we expand bad insurance, that will make rural healthcare worse and we do it for able-bodied adults who could get a job but choose not to work, and we make you pay for it twice!
But guess what? You lose and the politicians gain campaign donations.
This year was a disappointing year and one that is the beginning of the end for the Republican party in Mississippi. If Republican's can't govern on the principles of the Republican Party why would anyone continue to vote for them. I hate to say it but the Democrat party of Joe Biden is poised to take over our state.
So prepare yourself for a fast ride into the ditch because if the democrats have proven anything it is they know how to destroy a city, a state and a nation and the Mississippi Republicans are handing over the keys because of their own personal selfishness.
The only hope for our state is you. The conservative people of Mississippi must take a stand and vote out the Republicans who have adopted the Democrat philosophy of government.
Thanks for reading Dana Criswell! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

At approximately 1:00 am the final vote was cast for the last bill of the session. Never mind that the extended deadline had been exceeded and at midnight of March 31 all appropriation bills were dead. The Mississippi legislature once again ignored the law, the constitution and the good of the people and passed a bill that appropriated millions of dollars to "member projects". | | ---|---|--- | | | or ? ? from the desk ofDana Criswell You are our only hope! | | | May 15 | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- The 2023 legislative session ended on April 1 with the passage of the final bill of the legislative session. At approximately 1:00 am the final vote was cast for the last bill of the session. Never mind that the extended deadline had been exceeded and at midnight of March 31 all appropriation bills were dead. The Mississippi legislature once again ignored the law, the constitution and the good of the people and passed a bill that appropriated millions of dollars to "member projects". Actually that was a fitting end to a disappointing year in the Mississippi legislature. The Mississippi legislature has been run by majority Republicans since 2012 and has had a super majority for the past 8 year. But even with a super majority of Republicans, Mississippi politicians have continued to spend record amounts of the people's money. Thanks for reading Dana Criswell! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? This year was no different. The final count was a budget of over $7.6 billion, the largest in our state's history. The state is ending the 2022 fiscal year with over a $1 billion surplus. That means the state of Mississippi and it's Republican lead government has taken from the people of this state over a billion dollars more than it needed. But, even with a billion dollar surplus Republicans in the legislature refused to return any of the money, opting instead to spend every dime of your hard earned money on their pet projects to ensure their re-election. Earlier in the session during a Republican Caucus meeting Speaker Gunn brought up the idea of eliminating the state income tax. With a billion dollar surplus it would have been an easy task and one most would expect a group of Republicans to embrace. But that was not the case. At least 12 Republicans stood and begged the Speaker to kill a bill that eliminated the income tax. They expressed concerns that were drawn directly from the Democrat Party play-book and ultimately killed any chance of returning money to the people. One Republican actually stood up during the meeting and asked if he had somehow entered a Democrat party meeting. But the disappointments didn't end there. The majority Republican legislature continued its Democrat Party look-alike session by passing a bill that makes it difficult for Mississippians to purchase an Electric Vehicle. I'm not a huge fan of EVs but it is your decisions, not mine, if you want to buy a Tesla or an F450. But the Mississippi legislature decided to protect car dealerships my eliminating competition. Once again, you lose and the politicians gain campaign donations. One good thing the legislature did was attempt to fix a 2022 law that required a contractor license to do any work on a home. This misguided 2022 legislation was sold as "consumer protection" but in reality it made it technically illegal for you to work on your parents home. The legislature attempted to fix its 2022 error this year by repealing a portion of the bad law, but our Governor, who seems to have also decided the Democrat way is the best way, vetoed that bill because the Contractor Licensing Board urged him to protect them instead of the citizens. Once again, you lose and the politicians gain campaign donations. In a third strike our Governor, Lt. Governor, Speaker and a majority of Republicans decided to adopt the Democrats way of thinking and expanded medicaid. But wait, they say don't worry it was only a little bit! Medicaid expansion is not "free" money as the Democrats and their newly converted Republican friends call it. Medicaid is money from the federal government and a bunch of money from the state government. So is the federal money free? NO, it is money the federal government takes from you. So now we expand bad insurance, that will make rural healthcare worse and we do it for able-bodied adults who could get a job but choose not to work, and we make you pay for it twice! But guess what? You lose and the politicians gain campaign donations. This year was a disappointing year and one that is the beginning of the end for the Republican party in Mississippi. If Republican's can't govern on the principles of the Republican Party why would anyone continue to vote for them. I hate to say it but the Democrat party of Joe Biden is poised to take over our state. So prepare yourself for a fast ride into the ditch because if the democrats have proven anything it is they know how to destroy a city, a state and a nation and the Mississippi Republicans are handing over the keys because of their own personal selfishness. The only hope for our state is you. The conservative people of Mississippi must take a stand and vote out the Republicans who have adopted the Democrat philosophy of government. Thanks for reading Dana Criswell! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? Read all of Dana 's post and stay informed about politics in Mississippi ? | | | | | | | (C) 2023 Dana Criswell 530 George St., Suite 200, Jackson, MS ? ?

**ICYMI:** Earlier this month, Secretary Deb Haaland joined Rep. Gabe Vasquez in Southern New Mexico to announce a **$12.6 million grant for an advanced water treatment facility** in Alamogordo.
These funds will help address water quality and water shortages by updating technologying, turning contaminated or brackish water into safe drinking water.
As Secretary Haaland said at the announcement, **“Water is a sacred resource”** — especially in New Mexico.
As we strengthen our communities against climate change, we plan for this new facility to provide more safe water for our families, farmers, and residents.
**Bringing taxpayer dollars back to New Mexico to address our biggest issues — like drought resistance — is one of Gabe’s top priorities in Congress.** **[Gabe is delivering for NM-02, but he’s in a tough re-election campaign against a far-right extremist. Can you chip in $5 or more to keep Gabe fighting for us in Congress?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/63340584/406439138/-1912557965?refcode=fl_em_fr_20230510haaland_tv_180da_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMDE4MTYwYy02ZmYzLWVkMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1Fw7yf0yHj8YGuGzcIZOQXcbyqc6awRxLsKnCXO4U6c=)**
